name: OpenRouteService Routing and Geocoding API
what_it_does: Open-source routing and geocoding service with REST API — enabling developers to calculate routes for multiple transport modes (car, bike, foot, wheelchair), geocode addresses, compute isochrones, optimize routes, and build location-based services, powered by OpenStreetMap data with a free public API tier and self-hosted option.
use_cases: Calculating multi-modal routes via OpenRouteService API for transportation planning
Geocoding and reverse geocoding addresses via ORS API for location-based services
Computing accessibility isochrones via ORS API for service area and coverage analysis
Optimizing multi-stop routes via ORS Optimization API for delivery planning
Building wheelchair-accessible routing via ORS API for accessibility applications
not_for: Real-time traffic-aware routing (ORS is OSM-based without live traffic feeds)
Highly commercial production at scale without self-hosting (public API has rate limits)
Map tile rendering — ORS is routing/geocoding only
best_when: A developer, researcher, or non-profit needs free, open-source routing with specialty modes (wheelchair, HGV restrictions) — or wants to self-host routing without licensing fees using OpenStreetMap data.
avoid_when: You need real-time traffic, commercial production scale without self-hosting budget, or enterprise SLA.

auth.notes: API key via Authorization header or api_key query parameter. No OAuth2. No fine-grained scopes. No webhooks. JavaScript, Python, and R clients/SDKs. Public API free tier. Self-hosted option (Apache 2.0). Academic/research use encouraged. Separate endpoints for directions, geocoding, isochrones, matrix, optimization.

pricing.paid_tiers: Pro from €15/month; Business €99/month; Self-hosted free (open source)
pricing.notes: German academic project. Hosted by Heidelberg Institute for Geoinformation Technology (HeiGIT), University of Heidelberg. Apache 2.0 license. GitHub: GIScience/openrouteservice (1,400+ stars). Java-based. OpenStreetMap data. Public API provided as public service. Specialty: wheelchair routing, HGV restrictions, hazmat routing. Popular in academia, non-profits, and research. Used by: MSF (Doctors Without Borders) for humanitarian mapping, UNHCR for refugee routing. Route optimization via OR-Tools. Very popular among NGOs and open-source enthusiasts.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API with API key auth. Good OpenAPI documentation. JS, Python, R SDKs. No webhooks. GDPR compliant. German/academic hosting. Free public API 2,000 routing/day. Self-hosted option for unlimited. OpenStreetMap data basis. Specialty modes (wheelchair, HGV) are unique advantage.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: All routing requests fully idempotent. Geocoding idempotent for same query. Isochrone idempotent for same parameters. Pure read API — no state mutations.
known_agent_gotchas: 2,000 routing/day free limit — insufficient for production scale; self-host for unlimited
40 requests/minute burst limit — throttle agents for batch routing
OpenStreetMap data — wheelchair/HGV accuracy depends on OSM data completeness
Public API provided by academic institution — no commercial SLA
Self-hosted Java application — requires Docker or Java server setup

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. GDPR compliant. German academic hosting. API key via header or parameter. No OAuth2 or scope granularity. Open source — auditable Java code. Academic/non-profit operator — privacy-friendly by mission. Route data (waypoints) may contain location PII — handle appropriately. Self-hosted option for full data control.
